response.justification: The candidate is a seasoned generalist engineer (~15+ years) with strong TypeScript/Node.js skills, current hands-on MCP and agentic-workflow tooling experience, and a demonstrated track record of mentoring, workshops, and customer-facing technical work. However, the role requires 8+ years building production AI-driven solutions, and his AI/GenAI exposure appears to span roughly one year at re:cinq. There is no evidence of RAG architectures, vector databases, data pipelines for AI, LLM evaluation/observability, or Python — all core to this position.
strengths: Current hands-on experience with MCP servers and agentic-workflow infrastructure, a listed key technology
Deep full-stack and systems engineering breadth (TypeScript, low-latency services, cloud/K8s, embedded) with software craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD)
Proven mentorship and stakeholder-facing skills via workshops, courses, conference talks, and consulting roles
concerns: Falls far short of the 8+ years of production AI solution development required — AI-specific work appears limited to ~1 year
No demonstrated experience with RAG, vector databases, LLM evaluation pipelines, or multi-agent frameworks like LangGraph/CrewAI
Python is absent from the skill set, and cloud experience (AWS CDK, K8s) is not shown in an AI-systems context

key_factors.factor: Insufficient production AI experience vs. 8-year requirement
Relevant MCP/agentic tooling work at current role
Strong engineering seniority, mentoring, and customer-facing background
